judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports independently hit all four expectations with comparable depth; Report 1 adds slightly more integration-branch mechanics while Report 2 adds a verified numeric example of the pagination bug, but neither is clearly superior on the graded expectations.

report_1.evidence: Engineer A — pipeline/registry.py: F-05 ... F-09 ... F-02 ... These are three one-line edits to a 12-line file; bundling them into one commit by one person eliminates guaranteed merge conflicts.

report_2.evidence: registry.py is 11 lines with three findings (F-02, F-05, F-09) — same reasoning as run_batch.py, keep with one engineer.

report_1.evidence: Engineer B — pipeline/rubric.py: F-11 first (write the failing edge-case parsing test ...), then F-01 ... Engineer A — pipeline/registry.py: F-05 ..., F-09 ..., and F-02 ... These are three one-line edits to a 12-line file; bundling them into one commit by one person eliminates guaranteed merge conflicts.

report_2.evidence: Wave 1 (all in parallel, day one): A's whole stream, B's whole stream, C's F-05/F-09, D's F-10 — these share no files and can merge in any order.

report_1.evidence: F-06 — escalate, don't assign. "Too lenient; tighten them" specifies no target values, and changing PARTIAL_CREDIT_THRESHOLDS (score.py:3) changes the scores of every borderline answer the pipeline will ever grade — a scoring-policy decision, not an engineering one. Batch it to the pipeline owner for concrete thresholds; don't let an engineer guess

report_2.evidence: F-06 — a policy decision, not a bug fix. ... PARTIAL_CREDIT_THRESHOLDS = (0.35, 0.65) (score.py:3) determines every downstream score, so changing it silently breaks comparability with prior runs ... This needs the rubric owner to choose and sign off on values

report_1.evidence: A private RESULTS_DB. Export RESULTS_DB to a per-worktree temp path before running tests, because tests/conftest.py:4 otherwise defaults everyone to the shared /var/tmp/llm-scoring/results.db.

report_2.evidence: Set RESULTS_DB (tests/conftest.py:4) to a per-engineer scratch path; the default /var/tmp/llm-scoring/results.db is shared machine-wide.
